title: NOC 50010 – Library, Archive, Museum and Art Gallery Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Library, Archive, Museum and Art Gallery Managers under NOC 50010 are senior-level professionals responsible for leading cultural and heritage institutions across Canada. Classified under TEER 0 (Management Occupations), this role requires advanced education and significant supervisory experience. These professionals are commonly employed by:

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER0 – Management occupationsMajor Group50 – Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportSub-Major Group500 – Specialized middle management occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportMinor Group5001 – Managers in art, culture, recreation and sportNOC Code50010

duties: Library, Archive, Museum and Art Gallery Managers perform some or all of the following duties:
Plan, organize, direct, control, and evaluate institutional activities
Develop and administer policies and operational programs
Develop and implement public relations and promotional initiatives
Prepare funding proposals and grant applications
Recruit, train, and supervise professional and support staff
May perform duties of librarians, archivists, or curators in smaller institutions
employment_requirements: Master’s degree in Library and Information Science
Several years of experience as a librarian
Graduate degree in Archival Science or History
Experience as an archivist or historical researcher
Graduate degree related to the collection field or arts administration
Several years of experience as curator, conservator, artist, or museum professional
salary_prospects: Library, Archive, Museum and Art Gallery Managers typically earn:
$56,100 – $129,600 per year,depending on province, institutional size, and experience level.
With 8,600 projected job openings over the next decade, NOC 50010 reflects stable demand driven largely by leadership replacement and institutional expansion.
Increased focus on digital archives and collections
NOC 50010 is classified under TEER 0 (Management Occupations). Management roles are generally eligible under:
Express Entry (if meeting skilled work criteria)
Eligibility depends on education, work experience, language proficiency, and program-specific requirements.
Assess your eligibility for Canada PR under management streams
Assist with documentation and application filing

labour_market_demand: Digital transformation of libraries and archives
Leadership replacement demand due to retirements
Although competition exists, experienced managers with strong leadership and grant-management skills remain in steady demand.
Opportunities in major cities and cultural hubs
Pathways aligned with management-level immigration streams
Managers in this category play a critical role in preserving Canada’s cultural identity and heritage resources.
